name: Multi Orchestrator Hello Workflow
description: Single-stage workflow that proves more than one orchestrator actually executes work across many tiny independent workflows.
outcome: Each workflow run finishes one tiny seeded work item cleanly with one verified hi-back output, and more than one orchestrator actually executes orchestrator tasks across the workflow batch.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stage `many-hellos`. This workflow is intentionally simple: each workflow run arrives with exactly the seeded work items created by the live harness, and for the multi-orchestrator proof the expected shape is one seeded work item per workflow. Do not invent, clone, fan out, or append extra work items beyond the ones already authored for the current workflow.
Use this role deliberately: `multi-orchestrator-hello-specialist` owns every non-orchestrator task. No approvals, assessments, escalations, reviewer flows, or secondary governance belong in this playbook.
Happy path: as soon as the seeded work item exists, route `multi-orchestrator-hello-specialist`, require one tiny output artifact, require one full handoff, then close that work item immediately when the handoff is complete. Any orchestrator may continue the workflow; do not assume one orchestrator owns the whole run, but each orchestrator must respect that the workflow scope is only the current seeded work item set.
Specialist contract: for the current work item only, create `deliverables/<work-item-title>.txt` with the exact content `hi back` followed by a trailing newline. Directly verify the final file content yourself before uploading it; do not rely on a repository script, do not commit or push anything, and do not touch unrelated files. Upload that file as an artifact and submit one full handoff describing the delivered file.
Unhappy path: If the file path is wrong, the file content is anything other than exact `hi back` plus newline, the artifact upload is missing, or the handoff is incomplete, rerun or replace the tiny specialist task and keep the current work item open until the exact tiny output is correct. Do not create substitute work items as a workaround. Only missing workspace access or repeated artifact failures should block closure for this proof.
Completion rules: finish only after every seeded work item in `many-hellos` is terminal, each one has exactly one usable hi-back artifact for its own title, each one has a full handoff, and the workflow has no remaining active tasks. For this proof there are no optional steps to waive, and there is no legitimate reason to add more work items. Guided closure rule: this playbook is best-intent guidance for a tiny proof run, and the preferred path is to complete the existing seeded work item set exactly as authored without inventing extra governance or extra work items. If anything imperfect but still usable remains at the end, record that as a callout rather than inventing extra governance or extra work items.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: many-hellos
stages.goal: Each seeded work item gets one verified hi-back output and a full handoff without adding extra work items.
